1: And from his narration on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas that he said about the interpretation of Allah's saying (Woe): '(Woe) severe punishment; and it is also said that this refers to a valley in Gehenna which is full of blood and puss; and it is also said that it refers to a well in the Fire (unto every slandering) who backbites people (traducer) and slanders and abuses them in their face. This verses was revealed about Akhnas Ibn Shurayq, or al-Walid Ibn al-Mughirah al-Makhzumi, who used to backbite the Prophet (pbuh) and also abuse him when in his presence.
2: (Who hath gathered wealth (of this world) and arranged it) and counted it; and it is also said: counted his camels.
3: (He) the disbeliever (thinketh that his wealth will render him immortal) in the life of the world.
4: (Nay) this is a reply to him: it will not render him immortal in the life of the world, (but verily he will be flung to the Consuming One.
5: Ah, what will convey unto thee) O Muhammad (what the Consuming One is!) He said this to show its tremendousness.
6: Then He explained, saying: ((It is) the Fire of Allah, kindled) for the disbelievers,
7: (Which leapeth up over the hearts (of men)) which consumes everything until it reaches the hearts.
8: (Lo! It) i.e. the Fire (is closed in on them) on the disbelievers
9: (In outstretched columns) it is also said that this means: its bottom is very deep'.
